title: festuca idahoensis subsp. roemeri
intro: Festuca idahoensis subsp. roemeri is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is also known as Idaho fescue. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Festuca idahoensis subsp. roemeri include relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and treating wounds, and it can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for herbal use.
